rulings.Case Title: CHELSEA PACIFIC HOLDINGS, LP VS. MARIA FORTUNO ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 20, 2024 line 1. PLAINTIFF CHELSEA PACIFIC HOLDINGS, LP MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT /SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is DENIED. Moving party failed to shift its burden and show the absence of triable issues of fact with competent admissible evidence. For example, facts number 3 and 4 are supported by only 2 specific pages of Ex. A to Faidi declaration. Even if the burden was shifted, there are triable issues of fact, including facts 3, 4 and 6. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: JAMES BONG ENTERPRISE, INC. VS. MARY BONG ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 20, 2024 line 5. DEFENDANT MARY BONG, CHARLES CHUA, CHARLESTON CHUA, KINGSTON CHUA DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Complaint states a cause of action for unlawful detainer based on termination of license. Considering CC 1946.2 as a whole in conjunction with CCP 1161 and almost two centuries of case law regarding the requirements for termination of license, the allegations in the complaint are sufficient. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: HANCHEN ZHANG VS. EDWIN LAI ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 20, 2024 line 8. DEFENDANT EDWIN LAI, MARIKA REBECCA LOVELACE DEMURRER TO Amended COMPLAINT Hearing Required. No later than 9:00 a.m. on September 20, 2024 counsel for Plaintiff to file a declaration and produce a filed endorsed copy of the First Amended Complaint filed on August 2, 2024 containing purported "Tabs 1-3" and the allegations therein. Additionally, declaration shall include a complete citation for Baker v. Oakland (2018) referenced under TAB 3. A copy of the First Amended Complaint on file with the Court does not include those "tabs" or allegations included under those "tabs." Peter Deng stated under the penalty of perjury that Exhibit 1 attached to his September 13, 2024 declaration is a "true and correct copy of the First Amended Complaint filed on August 2, 2024" First amended complaint filed with the Court on August 2, 2024 contains 32 pages (The first page is page 1 of 4 of UD-100; the last page is a proof of service for First Amended Complaint). The purported true and correct copy of the First Amended Complaint attached to Deng declaration is 40 pages long. (The first page is page 1 of 4 of UD-100; the last page is a proof of service for First Amended Complaint; followed by TABS). The Court did not request any supplemental briefing. The Court did not request any alterations to the First Amended Complaint on file. The Court requested that Plaintiff point out exactly where in their previously filed First Amended Complaint the allegations may be located as neither the moving party nor the Court were able to locate such allegations. Missing or purportedly insufficient allegations formed the grounds of the demurrer. Opposition argues that the allegations already in the complaint were sufficient. The Court merely asked to highlight those already existing allegations, and tab them for ease of use as the First Amended Complaint is over 30 pages long. Therefore, since Peter Deng asserts under the penalty of perjury that First Amended Complaint in the form attached to his September 13, 2024 declaration was filed with the Court on August 2, 2024, Peter Deng shall provide a filed endorsed copy of such First Amended Complaint and re-affirm that the 40-page document including the "TABs" is the document he filed with the Court on August 2, 2024. No later that September 20, 2024 Defendant may file a declaration stating whether Plaintiff's service copy of August 2, 2024 First Amended Complaint included "Tabs 1-3." =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
